title: Marek Visits
description: Marek comes over for the first time. Something's broken, his pet questions, and a quick supermarket run.
context: Marek visits your flat for the first time. You show him around, offer him a drink, and head to the supermarket together.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Ahoj! Pekný byt. Toto je obývačka?
exchanges.translation: Hi! Nice flat. Is this the living room?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if this room is the living room. Confirm it.

choices.translation: Yes, this is the living room.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, toto je obývačka. — correct. Confirming the room name from the Items in My Flat lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Áno, toto je obývačka is correct since this is the living room.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Vidím, že máš mačku! Ako sa volá?
exchanges.translation: I see you have a cat! What's its name?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks your cat's name. It's called Micka. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Volá sa Micka. — correct. Volá sa + name, from the Pets lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Volá sa Micka (Its name is Micka) is correct since your cat's name is Micka.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Pekná mačka! Dáš si niečo na pitie?
exchanges.translation: Nice cat! Would you like something to drink?
exchanges.prompt: You're hosting — Marek hints he'd like a drink. What do you ask him?

choices.translation: Would you like something to drink?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Dáte si niečo na pitie? — correct. The polite host phrase from the Beverages lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Dáte si niečo na pitie? is the right phrase for offering a guest a drink.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Wait — Marek is asking you back. You'd like coffee. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct pattern — [drink], prosím. Any of these works to state your preference politely.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: [Drink], prosím is the pattern for stating what you'd like. Any of the four options works.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Hmm, počujem zvláštny zvuk. Niečo sa pokazilo?
exchanges.translation: Hmm, I hear a strange sound. Did something break?
exchanges.prompt: Marek notices a strange sound from the kitchen. Your fridge has been making noise. What do you say?

choices.translation: Yes, the fridge isn't working well.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, chladnička nefunguje dobre. — correct. Nefunguje, from the Items in My Flat lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Áno, chladnička nefunguje dobre (Yes, the fridge isn't working well) correctly describes the noise.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: To je škoda. A vidím, že chladnička je takmer prázdna.
exchanges.translation: That's a shame. And I see the fridge is almost empty.
exchanges.prompt: Marek notices you're low on groceries. What do you say?

choices.translation: I'm out of milk and cheese.
The bed is in the bedroom.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Došlo mi mlieko a syr. — correct. Došlo mi from the Beverages lesson, applied here to groceries too.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Došlo mi mlieko a syr (I'm out of milk and cheese) correctly explains the empty fridge.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Poďme spolu do obchodu! Pomôžem ti.
exchanges.translation: Let's go to the shop together! I'll help you.
exchanges.prompt: Marek offers to come with you to the supermarket. You agree. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Dobre, poďme! — correct. Simple agreement, ready to head out together.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Dobre, poďme! (Okay, let's go!) is the natural response to accept his offer.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Veľký nákup, alebo len trochu vecí?
exchanges.translation: A big shop, or just a few things?
exchanges.prompt: You only need a few items. What do you say, and which do you grab?

choices.translation: Just a little, I'll take a basket.
A lot, I'll take a trolley.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Len trochu, vezmem si košík. — correct. Trochu and košík, from the Supermarket lesson.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Len trochu, vezmem si košík (Just a little, I'll take a basket) matches needing only a few items.

exchanges.speaker: cashier
exchanges.prompt: At checkout, the cashier asks how you're paying. You want to pay by card. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Kartou. — correct and simple. From the Money lesson and reinforced in Supermarket.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Kartou (by card) is the correct one-word answer since you want to pay by card.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Skvelé, máme všetko! Bol to dobrý deň, ďakujem za hosťovanie.
exchanges.translation: Great, we have everything! It was a good day, thanks for having me.
exchanges.prompt: Marek thanks you for hosting him. How do you close out the day warmly?

choices.text: Ďakujem aj tebe, bolo to skvelé!
choices.translation: Thank you too, it was great!

exchanges.correctFeedback: Ďakujem aj tebe, bolo to skvelé! — perfect, warm close to a full day with Marek.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Ďakujem aj tebe, bolo to skvelé! (Thank you too, it was great!) is the warmest way to close out the day.

completionMessage: 🎉 Incredible! You showed Marek your flat, talked about your pet, offered him a drink, explained what was broken, and went shopping together — all in Slovak. You've completed Stage 1: Survival!
